summary: Recurring Services + Reorder Pack testing completed. Backend: 32/32 API tests passed (100%) after fixing datetime offset bug in SLA Alerts. Frontend: Admin SLA Alerts page fully working. Customer portal pages (Orders, Service Requests, Recurring Plans) are protected routes requiring authentication.

fixed_bugs.issue: TypeError: can't subtract offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
fixed_bugs.fix: Added helper functions make_aware() and safe_days_old() to handle timezone-naive datetimes from database

context_for_next_testing_agent: Recurring Services + Reorder Pack fully tested. All backend APIs working (32/32 tests passed). Fixed datetime offset bug in SLA Alerts. Admin SLA Alerts page verified working with 10 delayed orders displayed. Customer portal pages (Orders, Service Requests, Recurring Plans) have repeat buttons implemented but require customer user creation for full E2E testing. Customer user 'customer@test.com' does not exist in the users collection.

rca_of_the_issue.bug: SLA Alerts endpoint returning 500 Internal Server Error
rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: TypeError when subtracting timezone-naive datetime (from MongoDB) from timezone-aware datetime (datetime.now(timezone.utc))
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Added make_aware() and safe_days_old() helper functions to normalize datetime objects before comparison
